attributes.description: Alerts when the hard-parse ratio exceeds 10%. Indicates shared pool pressure or absent bind variables.
attributes.name: [Oracle OTel] Hard parse ratio high

investigation_guide.blob:
## Oracle hard parse ratio high

### What fired
`hard_parses / parse_calls > 10%` over the window.

### Why it matters
Hard parses are expensive — they fully re-plan a statement. High ratios indicate shared pool churn, ad-hoc SQL, or missing bind variables. This is a classic performance antipattern.

### Triage
1. Look at top literal-heavy SQL in ASH.
2. Check shared pool size.

### Remediation
- Force cursor sharing or adopt bind variables.
- Raise shared pool size.

### Tuning
- 10% threshold; tighten for OLTP.

esqlQuery.esql:
TS metrics-oracledbreceiver.otel-*
// hard_parses and parse_calls are co-located in Parses TSDB group; INCREASE for counter deltas
| WHERE oracledb.parse_calls IS NOT NULL
        AND oracledb.hard_parses IS NOT NULL
| STATS delta_hard = SUM(INCREASE(oracledb.hard_parses)),
        delta_total = SUM(INCREASE(oracledb.parse_calls))
        BY oracledb.instance.name, host.name
| WHERE delta_total > 0
// Ratio > 10% indicates shared pool pressure, ad-hoc SQL, or missing bind variables
// Adjust threshold (e.g. 0.05 for 5%) for stricter monitoring
| EVAL hard_parse_ratio_pct = ROUND(delta_hard / delta_total * 100.0, 1)
| WHERE hard_parse_ratio_pct > 10
| SORT hard_parse_ratio_pct DESC
